name: Zambeef Products PLC
description: Zambeef Products PLC is one of Zambia's largest integrated agro-processing companies, producing and retailing beef, chicken, pork, dairy, eggs, edible oils, flour, and stock feed. The company operates a farm-to-fork value chain with approximately 7,000 employees — making it one of Zambia's largest private employers. Listed on the LuSE Main Board, Zambeef is a major supplier to Zambian retailers, the mining sector, and exports to regional markets. The company is 46.3% institutionally owned with significant South African and UK institutional investor backing.

financials.debtNote: ZMW 2.4B debt against ZMW 6.37B equity — moderate leverage at D/E 0.38

analystNotes.note: Zambeef is the ultimate value trap or the deepest value on LuSE — and the truth depends on debt. The headline numbers are absurd: PE 3.55x, PB 0.09x, PS 0.07x, earnings yield 37.6%. A company with ZMW 8.06B revenue, ZMW 6.37B book value, and ZMW 226M profit trading at a ZMW 601M market cap. The market is pricing in distress — and the Altman Z-Score of 1.37 confirms it. ZMW 2.4B debt against ZMW 244M cash, interest coverage of just 2.05x, and negative FCF of -ZMW 105M. The 2.8% net margin on ZMW 8B revenue leaves almost no room for error. If the company can refinance debt and improve margins, it's a multi-bagger from these levels. If not, the Z-Score is right. The Piotroski F-Score of 6 is a rare bright spot. NOTE: companies.json INCORRECTLY labels ZMBF as 'Zambia Microfinance Bank' in Financial Services/ALT-M. This is wrong — ZMBF is Zambeef Products, agro-processing, Main Board. CORRECTION NEEDED.
analystNotes.author: Vituli Musukuma, PhD

riskFactors: Interest coverage only 2.05x — vulnerable to rate hikes
Debt ZMW 2.4B vs cash ZMW 244M — refinancing risk
Negative free cash flow (-ZMW 105M) — capex consuming cash
2.8% net margin — thin profitability on ZMW 8B revenue
Companies.json has WRONG name, sector, and market — data integrity risk
investmentThesis: Zambeef at PE 3.55x and PB 0.09x is either the cheapest stock on LuSE or a pre-restructuring zombie. The 0.09x book value suggests the market believes the assets are worth 9% of stated value — extreme skepticism. But ZMW 8B revenue and 7,000 employees make this a real operating company, not a shell. The Piotroski F-Score of 6 says the financial trend is improving. If management can refinance debt and improve margins beyond 2.8%, the re-rating potential is enormous. But until the balance sheet is de-risked, this is a special situations bet, not a core holding.
stance: SPECULATIVE DEEP VALUE — NEEDS DEBT RESTRUCTURING CATALYST
dataSources: companies.json — internal dashboard data (NAME AND SECTOR ARE INCORRECT)
dataQuality: HIGH — Based on FY2025 TTM data from StockAnalysis. CRITICAL NOTE: companies.json incorrectly identifies ZMBF as 'Zambia Microfinance Bank' in Financial Services/ALT-M. The correct identity is Zambeef Products PLC, an agro-processing company on the Main Board. This is a material data error requiring immediate correction.
